elements.sent-2411-001: The celebrated antiferromagnetic phase transition was realized in a most recent optical lattice experiment for 3D fermionic Hubbard model [Shao {\it et al ., Nature {\bf 632 , 267 (2024)].}}
elements.sent-2411-002: Despite the great achievement, it was observed that the AFM structure factor (and also the critical entropy) reaches the maximum around the interaction strength $U/t\simeq 11.75$, which is significantly larger than the theoretical prediction as $U/t\simeq 8$.
elements.sent-2411-003: Here we resolve this discrepancy by studying the interplay between the thermal entropy, density disorder and antiferromagnetism of half-filled 3D Hubbard model with numerically exact auxiliary-field quantum Monte Carlo simulations.
elements.sent-2411-004: We have achieved accurate entropy phase diagram, which allows us to simulate arbitrary entropy path on the temperature-interaction plane and to track the experimental parameters.
elements.sent-2411-005: We then find that above discrepancy can be quantitatively explained by the {\it entropy increase as enhancing the interaction in experiment, and together by the lattice {\it density disorder existing in the experimental setup.}}
elements.sent-2411-006: We furthermore investigate the entropy dependence of double occupancy, and predict its universal behaviors which can be used as useful probes in future optical lattice experiments.
elements.sent-2411-007: Quantum simulation combining ultracold atoms and optical lattice potentials~\cite{Anna2007,Bloch2008 has become an essential route to study the intriguing phenomena of strongly correlated systems.}
elements.sent-2411-008: One representative is the fermionic Hubbard model~\cite{Hubbard1963,Kanamori1963,Gutzwiller1963 , for which the optical lattice experiment, equipped with modern cooling, trapping and precision measurement techniques~\cite{Esslinger2010,Christian2017,Florian2020 , allows for exploring broader parameter regimes than those accessible in real materials, such as tuning the interaction strength.}}
elements.sent-2411-009: Since the early stage, the theoretical interests on Hubbard model~\cite{Arovas2022,Qin2022 range from quantum magnetism~\cite{LeBlanc2015,Boxiao2017,Xiao2023 to fermionic superfluidity~\cite{Sewer2002,Paiva2010,Fontenele2022 and more subtle $d$-wave superconductivity~\cite{Qin2020,Haoxu2024 , which are also the center pursuits of the optical lattice experiment nowadays.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-010: Especially in the past decade, the experimental studies of the antiferromagnetic (AFM) spin correlation in repulsive Hubbard model~\cite{Boll2016,Cheuk2016,Mazurenko2017,Cocchi2017,Muqing2023,Hart2015,Shao2024 has stood in line with or even surpassed the most advanced precision many-body computations~\cite{Qin2022 .}}
elements.sent-2411-011: An inspiring progress on this aspect is the observation of AFM phase transition (N\'{e el transition) in 3D Hubbard model with ultracold fermionic $^6$Li atoms in a large-scale and nearly uniform optical lattice ($\sim$$800,000$ sites)~\cite{Shao2024 , which has attracted widespread attention~\cite{Niaz2024,Miller2024 .}}}
elements.sent-2411-012: The transition from paramagnetic (PM) to N\'{e el AFM ordered phase was revealed via the critical scaling of the measured AFM structure factor with the critical exponent from the Heisenberg universality class~\cite{Campostrini2002 .}}
elements.sent-2411-013: However, several significant issues still exist in the interpretation for this experiment as well as its comparison with the theory.
elements.sent-2411-014: First, the experimental measurements followed irregular paths on the temperature-interaction ($T$-$U$) plane characterized the entropy instead of temperature~\cite{Shao2024 .}
elements.sent-2411-015: This complicates the direct comparisons with previous many-body numerical calculations of 3D Hubbard model~\cite{Staudt2000,Paiva2011,Paiva2015,Ibarra2020,Fanjie2024,Song2024a,Song2024b,Khatami2016,Werner2005,Raymond2007,Gorelik2012,Mauro2014,Rampon2024,Kozik2013,Lenihan2022,Garioud2024 , which typically presented results as a function of $T$ or $U$.}
elements.sent-2411-016: Second, at half filling, the maximum of the AFM structure factor and the critical entropy appears around $U/t\simeq 11.75$ in the experiment, in contrast to $U/t\simeq 8$ from the fixed-temperature calculations with unbiased auxiliary-field quantum Monte Carlo (AFQMC)~\cite{Song2024b .}
elements.sent-2411-017: To correctly understand this disagreement and its underlying physics is crucial for the next-step and quantitative studies of the metal-insulator crossover (MIC) in PM phase~\cite{Song2024a and more abundant phenomena with doping~\cite{Lenihan2022,Rampon2024 of 3D Hubbard model in the experiment.}}
elements.sent-2411-018: The other quantifiable issue in the experiment is the lattice density disorder inherited from the creation of the box trap potential~\cite{Shao2024 .}
elements.sent-2411-019: Its effect on various properties of the model, especially the AFM spin correlation, remains to be explored.
elements.sent-2411-020: In this work, we aim at bridging the gap between the experiment and theory, and resolve the above discrepancy, via performing numerically exact AFQMC calculations for half-filled 3D Hubbard model in a manner closely related to the experiment.
elements.sent-2411-021: In particular, we take thermal entropy as the central metric, and establish the full map of the entropy per particle ($\boldsymbol{s}$) on the $T$-$U$ plane.
elements.sent-2411-022: To mimic the experimental measurements, we compute the AFM structure factor ($S_{\rm AFM ^{zz}$) along representative entropy paths, and observe that the {\it entropy increase (not the trivial heating effect) can induce the peak location shifting of $S_{\rm AFM ^{zz}$ towards larger $U$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-023: We find that this phenomenon can be also contributed by the {\it density disorder .}
elements.sent-2411-024: We also investigate the universal behaviors of double occupancy versus $\boldsymbol{s}$ when cooling the system.
elements.figure-2411-001: \includegraphics[width=0.98\columnwidth]{Fig1_HeatMap.pdf} \caption{\label{fig:Fig01Entropy} Entropy phase diagram of repulsive 3D Hubbard model at half filling from our AFQMC calculations. Black solid lines are the isentropic curves with the $\boldsymbol{s}$ values (in units of $k_B$) marked on the lines. The residual finite-size effect is negligible. The N\'{e}el temperatures ($T_{N}$, red dashed line), and the positions of local maximum ($U_{\rm S1}$, light blue dashed line) and local minimum ($U_{\rm S2}$, white dashed line) of $\boldsymbol{s}$ at fixed temperatures from Ref.~\onlinecite{Song2024b} are also included. }
elements.sent-2411-025: The Hubbard Hamiltonian is $\hat{\mathcal{H =\sum_{\mathbf{k}\sigma}\varepsilon_{\mathbf{k c_{\mathbf{k}\sigma ^+c_{\mathbf{k}\sigma +U\sum_{\mathbf{i}\sigma [\hat{n _{\mathbf{i}\uparrow}\hat{n _{\mathbf{i}\downarrow -(\hat{n _{\mathbf{i}\uparrow +\hat{n _{\mathbf{i}\downarrow )/2]+\mu\sum_{\mathbf{i}\sigma (\hat{n _{\mathbf{i}\uparrow +\hat{n _{\mathbf{i}\downarrow )$, where $\hat{n _{\mathbf{i}\sigma}$ is the density operator with $\mathbf{i}$ labeling a site of the 3D optical lattice and $\sigma=\uparrow,\downarrow$ denoting the two hyperfine states of the ultracold atoms.}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-026: The kinetic energy dispersion reads $\varepsilon_{\mathbf{k =-2t\sum_{\alpha=x,y,z}\cos k_{\alpha}$ with the nearest-neighbor hopping $t$ and the momentum $k_{\alpha}$ defined in units of $2\pi/L$ (the lattice size is $N_s=L^3$).}}
elements.sent-2411-027: We set $t$ as energy unit, and focus on the model with repulsive interaction $U>0$ and at half filling ($\mu=0$).
elements.sent-2411-028: Both the algorithmic and simulation details of the AFQMC calculations can be referred in our previous work~\cite{Song2024a,Song2024b , in which we compute the N\'{e el temperatures and explore the MIC physics in PM phase of half-filled 3D Hubbard model from a purely theoretical aspect.}}
elements.sent-2411-029: In this work, we take a step further to enable our numerical simulations to shake hands with the experiment.
elements.sent-2411-030: It is the thermal entropy $\boldsymbol{s}$ instead of temperature which is directly controlled and measured in optical lattice experiments.
elements.sent-2411-031: In numerics, we first compute $\boldsymbol{s}$ versus $U$ for an ensemble of fixed temperatures (with sufficiently small interval)~\cite{Song2024b , and then we perform an interpolation for the numerical data to achieve the full map of $\boldsymbol{s}$ on the $T$-$U$ plane as presented in Fig.~\ref{fig:Fig01Entropy .}}
elements.sent-2411-032: Our results are surely more compact and reliable than the previously reported isentropic properties for half-filled 3D Hubbard model~\cite{Werner2005,Raymond2007 , which applied numerical methods with uncontrolled approximations.}
elements.sent-2411-033: As expected, the AFM phase (below the $T_{N}$ line as N\'{e el transition) has the globally small entropy due to AFM long-range order.}
elements.sent-2411-034: In comparison, $\boldsymbol{s}$ in PM phase exhibits significantly more abundant structures which is closely related to the MIC~\cite{Song2024a,Song2024b and effective Heisenberg physics.}
elements.sent-2411-035: The most prominent feature of the entropy map in Fig.~\ref{fig:Fig01Entropy is the non-monotonic behavior of the isentropic curves $T_i(U)$, along which $\boldsymbol{s (T_i(U), U)=\boldsymbol{s _i$ as a constant.}}}
elements.sent-2411-036: With enhancing $U$, The isentropic temperature $T_i(U)$ first decreases, then increases in the middle, and finally decays again.
elements.sent-2411-037: This feature can be understood from the fixed-temperature behavior of $\boldsymbol{s}$ versus $U$ as follows.
elements.sent-2411-038: The total derivative of $\boldsymbol{s (T_i(U), U)=\boldsymbol{s _i$ as $d\boldsymbol{s =(\partial\boldsymbol{s /\partial T)dT + (\partial\boldsymbol{s /\partial U)dU=0$ yields \begin{equation}\begin{aligned} \label{eq:DiffEqu0} \frac{\partial\boldsymbol{s}}{\partial U}\Big|_{T=T_i} = -\frac{c(T_i)}{T_i}\frac{{\rm d}T_i(U)}{{\rm d}U}, \end{aligned}\end{equation} with $c(T)=T\times(\partial\boldsymbol{s /\partial T)$ denoting the specific heat which is positive for $T>0$.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-039: This equality clearly shows that the slope sign of $T_i(U)$ is opposite to that of $\boldsymbol{s (T,U)$ versus $U$ at $T=T_i$, and their extreme points as ${\rm d T_i/{\rm d U=0$ and $(\partial\boldsymbol{s /\partial U)|_{T=T_i =0$ should coincide at the same $U$ value.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-040: It was previously shown~\cite{Song2024b that, $\boldsymbol{s (T,U)$ at fixed $T$ first increases in Fermi liquid regime and reaches a local maximum ($U_{\rm S1}$ in Fig.~\ref{fig:Fig01Entropy ), and then decreases as entering the MIC regime and develops a local minimum ($U_{\rm S2}$ in Fig.~\ref{fig:Fig01Entropy ), and finally increases to $\ln(2)$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-041: This corresponds to $+$, $-$, $+$ as the signs of $(\partial\boldsymbol{s /\partial U)|_{T=T_i}$ in the three regimes separated by $U_{\rm S1}$ and $U_{\rm S2}$, which renders $-$, $+$, $-$ signs of ${\rm d T_i/{\rm d U$ respectively according to Eq.~(\ref{eq:DiffEqu0 ), and thus explains the shape of $T_i(U)$ curves.}}}}

elements.figure-2411-002: \includegraphics[width=0.98\columnwidth]{Fig2_IsoAFM.pdf} \caption{\label{fig:Fig02SafmAndT} Numerical results of (a) AFM structure factor $S_{\rm AFM}^{zz}$ and (b) the temperatures $T/t$ as a function of $U/t$, along representative entropy paths. Beside the isentropic path with $\boldsymbol{s}\equiv0.27k_B$ (green solid line), the other three paths (red, blue and yellow solid lines) follow the relation $\boldsymbol{s}/k_B=\ln(2) - A[1-(U/B-1)/\sqrt{(U/B-1)^2+C^2}]$ with three different sets of $A,B,C$ parameters, and are plotted in the inset of (a). The $B$ parameter and the initial entropy $\boldsymbol{s}(U=0)$ for the paths are illustrated in the legend. elements.figure-2411-003: \includegraphics[width=0.95\columnwidth]{Fig3_Disorder.pdf} \caption{\label{fig:Fig03DisOrder} Comparisons of AFM structure factor $S_{\rm AFM}^{zz}$ results versus $U/t$ within periodic boundary conditions (``PBC'', without disorder) and lattice {\it density disorder} (``Disorder'') for $T/t=0.30$ and $T/t=0.36$. The vertical shading bands denote the peak locations of $S_{\rm AFM}^{zz}$. The inset (a) plots the comparisons of kinetic energy per site $e_K/t$ and double occupancy $D$ between ``PBC'' and ``Disorder'' calculations for $T/t=0.30$. elements.sent-2411-099: In summary, we have systematically studied the interplay between the thermal entropy, density disorder and AFM properties in half-filled 3D Hubbard model following the manner of the most recent optical lattice experiment~\cite{Shao2024 .}
elements.sent-2411-100: We have established the full entropy map on $T$-$U$ plane for the model, based on which we have resolved the discrepancy between the experiment and theory on the peak antiferromagnetism.
elements.sent-2411-101: More specifically, we have found that the {\it entropy increase in experimental measurements during increasing interaction and {\it density disorder both induce the peak location shifting of AFM structure factor towards stronger interaction.}}
elements.sent-2411-102: We have also presented predictions for the universal behaviors of double occupancy versus entropy to probe various properties of the system, which can serve as useful guidance for future experimental studies.
elements.sent-2411-103: Our work have bridged the gap between the most recent experiment and the cutting-edge quantum many-body computations, and have built a paradigm for the benchmark between them, by taking the entropy as a central role and including the density disorder as a realistic issue.
elements.sent-2411-104: This paradigm is important and applicable to the benchmark studies on many other exotic properties of various versions of Hubbard model~\cite{Arovas2022,Qin2022 between experiment and theory.}
elements.sent-2411-105: This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (under Grants No. 12047502, No. 12204377, and No. 12275263), the Innovation Program for Quantum Science and Technology (under Grant No. 2021ZD0301900), the Natural Science Foundation of Fujian province of China (under Grant No. 2023J02032), and the Youth Innovation Team of Shaanxi Universities.
elements.section-2411-001: The heat maps of AFM structure factor and double occupancy
elements.sent-2411-106: In Fig.~\ref{fig:FigS1HeatMap , we present the numerical results of the full maps of antiferromagnetic (AFM) structure factor $S_{\rm AFM ^{zz}$ and double occupancy $D$ on $T$-$U$ plane for 3D half-filled Hubbard model.}}
elements.sent-2411-107: As expected, the finite-size $S_{\rm AFM ^{zz}$ result is much bigger inside the AFM ordered phase.}
elements.sent-2411-108: Note that $S_{\rm AFM ^{zz}$ should diverge in AFM phase and gradually saturate to finite values in paramagnetic (PM) phase as approaching $L=\infty$.}
elements.sent-2411-109: The contour lines of double occupancy reveal that this quantity has very weak temperature dependence, especially in PM phase.
elements.sent-2411-110: Moreover, the shape of contour lines of $D$ bends a little bit when crossing the N\'{e el transitions.}
elements.figure-2411-005: \includegraphics[width=0.485\columnwidth]{FigS1_AFMHeatMap.pdf} \includegraphics[width=0.485\columnwidth]{FigS2_DouHeatMap.pdf} \caption{\label{fig:FigS1HeatMap} The heat map of AFM structure factor $S_{\rm AFM}^{zz}$ and double occupancy $D$ on $T$-$U$ plane for 3D half-filled Hubbard model from our AFQMC simulations. For $S_{\rm AFM}^{zz}$, the results consist of $L=12$ data for $T\le0.35$ and converged results (to thermodynamic limit) for $T\ge0.40$. For $D$, the residual finite-size effect is negligible. The red dashed line plots the N\'{e}el temperatures $T_{N}$. }
elements.figure-2411-006: \includegraphics[width=0.77\columnwidth]{FigS3_AFMEntropy.pdf} \caption{\label{fig:FigS2Scaling} Critical scaling of $S_{\rm AFM}^{zz}$ versus the entropy per particle $\boldsymbol{s}/k_B$ for $U/t=10$ with $L=10$ and $L=16$, using the relation $S_{\rm AFM}^{zz}=a(\boldsymbol{s}/\boldsymbol{s}_{\rm N}-1)^{-\gamma}+b$ with $\gamma=1.396$ as the critical exponent from the Heisenberg universality class~\cite{Campostrini2002}. The inset is the semi-log plot of $(S_{\rm AFM}^{zz}-b)$ versus $(\boldsymbol{s}/\boldsymbol{s}_{\rm N}-1)$, with dashed lines denoting the fitting curves.}
elements.section-2411-002: The critical scaling of AFM structure factor versus entropy
elements.sent-2411-111: In Fig.~\ref{fig:FigS2Scaling , we present the critical scaling of AFM structure factor $S_{\rm AFM ^{zz}$ versus the thermal entropy per particle $\boldsymbol{s}$ for $U/t=10$ with $L=10$ and $L=16$ systems.}}
elements.sent-2411-112: We adopt the formula $S_{\rm AFM ^{zz =a(\boldsymbol{s /\boldsymbol{s _{\rm N -1)^{-\gamma +b$ for the fitting using the numerical data in PM phase, following the manner in the experiment~\cite{Shao2024 .}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-113: The results indeed fits the scaling relation quite well, and the critical extropy $\boldsymbol{s _{\rm N /k_B$ from the fitting is comparable to numbers presented in the main text.}}
elements.sent-2411-114: We also note that the critical extropy extracted from the experimental data~\cite{Shao2024 is significantly smaller than our numerical results, such as $\boldsymbol{s _{\rm N /k_B=0.27(1)$ for $U/t=11.75$ in experiment comparing to $\boldsymbol{s _{\rm N /k_B=0.33(1)$ for $U/t=10$ in our numerics.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-115: This disagreement might be attributed to two reasons.
elements.sent-2411-116: First, the experiment data process used the initial single-particle entropy (before loading the optical lattice and adding the interaction), and the experimental measurements with increasing $U$ should have entropy increase as discussed in the main text.
elements.sent-2411-117: Second, the existence of lattice density disorder in the experimental setup should also suppress the N\'{e el transition temperatures of the system, which consequently reduces the critical entropy.}
